quiz.title: AP Calculus AB Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: Applications of Derivatives — Motion, Rates, and Linear Approximation (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: Calculus

questions.question: The position of a particle moving along a line is given by $s(t) = \frac{t^2}{t+1}$, where $s$ is in meters and $t$ is in seconds ($t \geq 0$). Find the velocity of the particle at $t = 2$ seconds.
Find the slope of the tangent line to the curve $f(x) = x^2 \ln x$ at the point where $x = 1$.
questions.explanation: Using product rule: $f'(x) = 2x \ln x + x^2 \cdot \frac{1}{x} = 2x \ln x + x$. At $x = 1$: $f'(1) = 2(1)(0) + 1 = 1$.

questions.question: The side length of an equilateral triangle is increasing at a rate of 3 cm/s. How fast is the area increasing when the side length is 8 cm? (Area $A = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}s^2$)
The profit from selling $x$ units of a product is $P(x) = -0.2x^2 + 80x - 1000$. Find the marginal profit when $x = 150$.
questions.explanation: Marginal profit $P'(x) = -0.4x + 80$. At $x = 150$: $P'(150) = -0.4(150) + 80 = -60 + 80 = 20$ dollars per unit. (Selling the 151st unit will increase profit by approximately $20.)

questions.question: A particle moves along a line with position $s(t) = 2t^3 - 15t^2 + 24t + 10$. At what times is the particle at rest?
questions.explanation: Velocity $v(t) = s'(t) = 6t^2 - 30t + 24 = 6(t^2 - 5t + 4) = 6(t-1)(t-4)$. Set $v(t) = 0$: The particle is at rest at $t = 1$ second and $t = 4$ seconds.

questions.question: Find the equation of the tangent line to $f(x) = \sqrt{x+3}$ at the point $(1, 2)$. Use it to estimate the value of $\sqrt{3.9}$.
questions.explanation: $f'(x) = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x+3}}$, slope at $x = 1$ is $\frac{1}{4} = 0.25$. Tangent: $y - 2 = 0.25(x - 1)$ or $y = 0.25x + 1.75$. For $\sqrt{3.9}$, $x = 0.9$: $\sqrt{3.9} \approx 0.25(0.9) + 1.75 = 1.975$. (Actual value is approximately 1.9748).

questions.question: Using the position function $s(t) = \frac{t^2}{t+1}$, find the acceleration of the particle at $t = 3$ seconds.
The radius of a tree trunk (in cm) is given by $r(t) = 5 + 2\ln(t+1)$, where $t$ is in years. Find the instantaneous rate at which the radius is growing at $t = 4$ years.

A ladder 10 feet long is leaning against a wall. The bottom of the ladder is sliding away from the wall at a rate of 1 ft/s. How fast is the top of the ladder sliding down the wall when the bottom is 6 feet from the wall?
questions.explanation: Given $x^2 + y^2 = 100$. Differentiate: $2x\frac{dx}{dt} + 2y\frac{dy}{dt} = 0$. When $x = 6$, $y = 8$. Given $\frac{dx}{dt} = 1$: $6(1) + 8\frac{dy}{dt} = 0$ → $\frac{dy}{dt} = -\frac{6}{8} = -0.75$ ft/s. (Negative indicates sliding down.)

questions.question: If $f'(x) = x^2 - 4$, determine whether $f$ is increasing or decreasing at $x = 3$. Also, find the critical points of $f$.
options: Increasing at x=3; critical points at x=±2
Decreasing at x=3; critical points at x=±2
Increasing at x=3; critical points at x=±4
Decreasing at x=3; critical points at x=±4
questions.explanation: $f'(3) = 9 - 4 = 5 > 0$, so $f$ is increasing at $x = 3$. Critical points occur when $f'(x) = 0$: $x^2 - 4 = 0$ → $x = \pm 2$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered applications of derivatives.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand most derivative applications.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on setting up problems correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review basic differentiation rules and their applications.
